comment.body: Hi, I am curious how you actually use OD charts. Do you benefit from OD subscription at all? From my limited understanding, Alma posts are good enough and the OD intraday data doesn't seem to offer much additional edge. Maybe just I don't know how to use OD.
comment.raw_body: Hi, I am curious how you actually use OD charts. Do you benefit from OD subscription at all? From my limited understanding, Alma posts are good enough and the OD intraday data doesn't seem to offer much additional edge. Maybe just I don't know how to use OD.

comment.body:
i mainly watch the breakdown by strikes of the 0DTE market maker positioning during intraday price action... clusters of either negative positioning or positive positioning typically give me a rough range as to where the price action may bounce/reject... it's not flawless but it does provide some edge for me when i can combine it with volume profile and liquidity sweeps

i also have the gamma and charm charts up to get a general look at the gamma environment we may be in... as for charm, i often only reference it towards the end of the day, if at all... sometimes if the charm values are high earlier on in the day i'll give it some attention but it generally doesn't impact my decision making process

i also find it nice to see if a large iron condor is on, or risk reversals, flies as they could impact how dealers hedge positions throughout the day (though i'm much less experienced on this front)
comment.raw_body:

i also have the gamma and charm charts up to get a general look at the gamma environment we may be in... as for charm, i often only reference it towards the end of the day, if at all... sometimes if the charm values are high earlier on in the day i'll give it some attention but it generally doesn't impact my decision making process

comment.body:
customer buys an SPX call,
dealer becomes short delta,
he then buys ES futures.

ES moves first, cash basket lags, arbitrage opp, index catches up
